title: ArAnnotationReview
description: One reviewer's verdict on one prose field of one Aristotelian record. Per aristotelian-probe-sketch-01 APA1 + production-format-sketch-11 PFS11-AR1..AR6. Shape parallels schema/lowering/annotation_review.json in verdict vocabulary but targets an Aristotelian record's prose field (identified by target_kind + target_id + field triple) rather than a Lowering's annotation. Pair-consistency — field values constrained by target_kind per FIELDS_BY_TARGET_KIND — enforced via allOf/if-then-else per PFS11-X1. Optional id field kept per Python (APA1 pre-date PFS9 annotation_review; convergence across namespaces banked as PFS11 OQ5).

reviewer_id.description: Stable identifier for the reviewer (human handle or LLM-probe identifier). PFS11-AR2.

reviewed_at_τ_a.description: Author-time τ_a at which the review was recorded. PFS11-AR2.

target_kind.description: Closed enum matching core/aristotelian.py VALID_REVIEW_TARGET_KINDS. Each reviewable Aristotelian record kind appears here. If a future dialect-record kind acquires a reviewable prose field, this enum expands. PFS11-AR3.

target_id.description: Intra-dialect record id of the reviewed record. Referential-integrity audit banked as PFS11 OQ3. PFS11-AR2.

field.description: Closed enum matching VALID_REVIEW_FIELDS. Each token names the one reviewable prose field on its target kind — per APA1's one-field-per-target rule. Pair-consistency with target_kind enforced via allOf per PFS11-X1. PFS11-AR4.

verdict.description: Closed enum matching Python's VERDICT_* constants (duplicated in aristotelian.py to avoid a lowering.py import). Same four-value shape as lowering/annotation_review.json — the two AnnotationReview records share verdict vocabulary. approved = fine as-is; needs-work = revision requested with specific concern; rejected = remove/replace; noted = read but no position taken. PFS11-AR5.

anchor_τ_a.description: Snapshot of the encoding-level τ_a (Aristotelian records don't carry record-level τ_a per APA1) at review time. Required per PFS7 convention — Python always carries the default-0 value; schema requires it. If the encoding's τ_a advances past this value, downstream tooling surfaces the review as stale. PFS11-AR1 + PFS11-AR2.

comment.description: Optional reviewer prose. Python default None → omitted from dump; any set string emits unconditionally. PFS11-AR6.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for external cross-reference. Python default None → omitted when None. Differs from lowering/annotation_review.json (no id field). PFS8 OQ4 closure — kept optional per Python; convergence across namespaces banked as PFS11 OQ5. PFS11-AR6.
